tasks.label: Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished

memory.summary: I'm almost done with the assignment, and since nothing else is urgent, I can focus on finishing this without rushing. My energy levels are decent enough to handle the final 10% calmly.

The deadline passed while I was at 95% completion, turning my planned calm finish into an unfinished submission. This directly conflicts with my value of relationships since I now owe an explanation to the professor and teammates.
My hard stop at 13:30 forced an incomplete submission, directly damaging my relationship trust with the professor. I must now renegotiate this boundary violation rather than hide it.
Finishing the last 3% of this assignment feels like a small victory, but it only deepens my debt to the professor. I must immediately renegotiate this breach of trust rather than hiding the delay.

desires: To immediately renegotiate with the professor to restore trust before I am perceived as unreliable or dismissive of their time.
To finish the assignment now so I stop carrying the guilt of an incomplete submission that damages my standing in the cohort.
To avoid the exhaustion of a sleepless night caused by anxiety over explaining away this boundary violation.

goals.description: Submit the assignment immediately to halt the anxiety spiral, then draft a direct message to prof_assignment explaining the hard stop at 13:30 to renegotiate the deadline before they assume incompetence.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) demands I protect my reputation with Prof Assignment above all else; letting them think I'm unreliable is worse than a late submission. My assertive style requires me to take decisive action now rather than negotiating with myself about 'finishing' the last 5% when the window feels closed. Submitting stops the bleeding on energy so I can be present for Cohort 3 later.
